summary:
At birth, most primitive infant reflexes such as the Moro, Babinski, and startle responses typically manifest; however, the Landau reflex appears later between 4-6 months old due to its delayed emergence compared to other neonatal reflexes. Therefore,
\boxed{\text{The exception would be A}} This summary captures both critical aspects—the timing differences among these specific newborn reflexes—and identifies why only one option fits outside this initial window shortly after childbirth. ```plaintext
At birth, typical neonatal reflexes include the Moro, Babinski, and startle responses, while the Landau reflex develops much later starting from about 4-6 months. 
Therefore, \boxed{\text{the exception is A}}
```
